settings_item_max_versions_per_file_desc: The maximum number of historical versions kept for each file. Older versions are cleaned up once the limit is exceeded.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_source: The largest uploaded source archive file allowed to enter online extraction. Lower values reduce metadata parsing pressure and decompression workload.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_stagin: The largest temporary disk footprint allowed while extracting an archive, counted as downloaded source archive bytes plus extracted staging bytes before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_uncomp: The largest total uncompressed file bytes accepted inside one archive before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entrie: The maximum number of archive entries accepted during online extraction. This limits metadata-heavy archives with many tiny files or directories.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_files_: The maximum number of regular file entries accepted inside one archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_direct: The maximum number of directory paths accepted or implicitly created while extracting one archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_depth_: The deepest normalized archive entry path accepted during online extraction.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_path_b: The maximum UTF-8 byte length accepted for a normalized archive entry path.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_compre: The maximum total uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for an archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entry_: The maximum per-file uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for archive entries.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_durati: The longest wall-clock runtime allowed for one online extraction task before the server stops it.
settings_item_archive_preview_enabled_de: Master switch for read-only archive manifest preview. Keep this off unless you explicitly want the server to scan archive metadata for previews.
settings_item_archive_preview_user_enabl: Allow signed-in users to preview archive manifests for personal and team files. The master archive preview switch must also be enabled.
settings_item_archive_preview_share_enab: Allow public share pages to preview archive manifests after password and share-scope checks. Keep this off if shared archive metadata should stay private.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_source: The largest source archive file accepted for read-only preview. Lower values reduce temp-file and metadata scanning pressure.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_entrie: The maximum number of archive entries accepted for read-only preview.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_manife: The largest serialized manifest returned to clients. Larger manifests are truncated before response and cache storage.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_durati: The longest wall-clock runtime allowed for one read-only archive preview scan.
settings_item_archive_compress_enabled_d: Allow users to create ZIP archives as new files in personal and team workspaces. Archive downloads and extraction stay controlled separately.
settings_item_archive_download_user_enab: Allow signed-in users to download selected personal and team files as ZIP archives. Disable this to block batch ZIP download tickets for workspace files.
settings_item_archive_download_share_ena: Allow public share visitors to download selected shared files as ZIP archives after password and share-scope checks. Disable this if shared folders should not be exportable as batch ZIP files.
settings_item_archive_build_max_entries_: The maximum number of expanded file and directory entries allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_total_so: The maximum total source file bytes allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_temp_byt: The maximum estimated or actual ZIP output bytes allowed while creating archives, limiting temporary disk and streaming resource use.
settings_item_offline_download_engine_de: The server-side link import engine. Use builtin for the self-contained Rust downloader, or aria2 to delegate downloads to an administrator-managed aria2 JSON-RPC daemon.
settings_item_offline_download_engine_re: Configure which server-side link-import engines are available, whether they are enabled, and the order they are tried. New link-import tasks fail when every engine is disabled.
offline_download_engine_editor_disabled_: No link-import engine is enabled. New link-import tasks will fail.
offline_download_engine_editor_invalid_j: The link-import engine registry is not valid JSON.
offline_download_engine_editor_invalid_v: The link-import engine registry version must be {{version}}.
offline_download_engine_editor_builtin_d: AsterDrive performs HTTP/HTTPS downloads inside the app process. It needs no extra service and uses the global size, speed, concurrency, and timeout limits.
offline_download_engine_editor_aria2_des: Delegates the actual download to an administrator-managed aria2 service. Requires RPC URL and secret configuration, and is useful when a dedicated downloader should handle connections, splitting, and low-speed aborts.
offline_download_engine_validation_error: Fix the link-import engine configuration first.
settings_item_offline_download_max_file_: The largest HTTP/HTTPS source file size accepted for server-side link imports. Tune this with the request timeout; 1 GiB in 600 seconds needs about 1.7 MiB/s.
settings_item_offline_download_max_mb_pe: The maximum server-side HTTP/HTTPS download speed in MB/s for each link import task. Use 0 for unlimited. If this is below the size/timeout throughput target, large imports may time out.
settings_item_offline_download_max_concu: The maximum number of link import download tasks the server may execute at the same time.
settings_item_offline_download_request_t: The maximum number of seconds allowed for one outbound HTTP/HTTPS download request. Tune this with the size and speed caps; 1 GiB in 600 seconds needs about 1.7 MiB/s.
settings_item_offline_download_temp_dir_: Absolute path used to stage link-import files. AsterDrive and external downloaders such as aria2 must be able to access and write the same path; leave blank to use the default server temp directory.
settings_item_offline_download_aria2_rpc: The aria2 JSON-RPC endpoint used when the link import engine is aria2, such as http://127.0.0.1:6800/jsonrpc.
The aria2 JSON-RPC secret. It is stored as a sensitive setting and sent to aria2 as token authentication.
settings_item_offline_download_aria2_req: The timeout in seconds for one aria2 JSON-RPC request. The full download duration is still controlled by the link import request timeout.
settings_item_offline_download_aria2_spl: The aria2 split option for each link import task. This is an administrator-controlled safe subset, not arbitrary aria2 option passthrough.
settings_item_offline_download_aria2_max: The aria2 max-connection-per-server option for each link import task.
settings_item_offline_download_aria2_low: The aria2 lowest-speed-limit option in bytes per second. Use 0 to disable this aria2-side abort threshold.
settings_item_thumbnail_max_source_bytes: The largest source file size still allowed to enter thumbnail generation. Larger files are skipped so oversized originals do not overload thumbnail jobs.
settings_item_thumbnail_max_dimension_de: The maximum generated thumbnail width or height in pixels. Lower values reduce thumbnail storage, bandwidth, and processing cost.
settings_item_image_preview_max_dimensio: The maximum generated image preview width or height in pixels. Raise this for sharper large-screen previews, or lower it to reduce storage and bandwidth.
settings_item_media_metadata_enabled_des: Allow the backend to parse and cache basic image, audio, and video metadata by blob. When disabled, the music player relies only on browser-side fallback parsing.
settings_item_media_metadata_max_source_: The largest source file size still allowed to enter backend metadata extraction. Larger media files are skipped to limit background task reads.
settings_item_media_processing_registry_: Choose which media processors are enabled and bind extensions as needed. Built-in AsterDrive processors act as the final fallback when enabled.
settings_item_frontend_image_preview_pre: Default image source used by the web preview dialog. Original-first downloads the original immediately; preview-first loads only the generated medium image until the user chooses to show the original.
settings_item_avatar_dir_desc: Local filesystem directory used for uploaded avatar files. Absolute paths are used as-is. Relative paths are resolved under the server's ./data directory.
settings_item_avatar_max_upload_size_byt: The largest avatar file users are allowed to upload. Oversized uploads are rejected, and requests are still bounded by the server's hard payload limit.
